title: Ludovia integrifolia
taxonomy: Ludovia integrifolia is a species of plant in the family Aponogetonaceae. The genus Ludovia belongs to the monocot order Alismatales. Ludovia integrifolia is native to South America, specifically in the mountainous regions. It is a type of aquatic plant that grows in still or slow-moving water. Ludovia integrifolia is further classified under the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ludovia integrifolia is a species of liverwort that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat infections and promote wound healing.
